posts.caption:
The only M1 certified U-shape seating frame on the market — and it shows in the details.

Built from laser-cut precision steel with a textured black powder coat, our U-Shape Seating Frame gives you four to five passenger seats, integrated 3-point seatbelts, ISOFIX points for child seats, and a full-width sleeping platform — all from one piece of furniture.

It fits the VW T5, T6, T6.1 and Ford Transit Custom, and it has been independently tested to ECE R14.09 — the same standard as factory-fitted passenger seating.

Add under-bed drawer storage, cushion boards, and front-facing aluminium panels to complete the build.

Starting from £1,850. See the full spec at the link below.
posts.imageSuggestion: Photo of U-shape frame installed in a VW Transporter, showing the full seating layout with cushions fitted

posts.caption:
Rock and roll bed or U-shape seating? The answer depends on more than just space.

Here is how they compare on the things that matter most:

✅ Passenger capacity — rock and roll typically seats 2-3. A U-shape seats 4-5.
✅ Sleeping platform — both convert to sleep, but U-shape gives a wider, more generous layout.
✅ ISOFIX compatibility — most rock and roll beds do not include ISOFIX. Our U-shape frame does.
✅ M1 certification — essential for legal motor caravan registration. Always ask for ECE R14.09 documentation, not just a general claim.
✅ Cost — rock and roll is the lower-cost option. U-shape costs more but gives more functionality.

If you regularly travel with family or friends, the U-shape layout changes the feel of the trip completely. It is a proper living space, not just a seat that folds flat.

Full comparison guide now on the blog — link in bio.
posts.imageSuggestion: Side-by-side graphic showing rock and roll bed layout vs U-shape layout from above, with labels for seat count and sleeping area

posts.caption:
Some campervans are built for two. Others are built for the whole family.

The moment you start travelling with kids, friends, or anyone beyond a couple, the rear layout of your van starts to matter in a very different way. You want somewhere to sit together. Somewhere to eat. Somewhere the kids can be safely strapped in.

That is what we designed the U-shape around — not just a place to sleep, but a place to live.

Where is the van taking you this spring? Drop a comment below — we would love to hear what you have planned.

🚐 ROAM Systems — M1 certified campervan furniture, made in the UK.
